title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – Q1'26
content:
The credit union financial position continues to be supported by exceptionally strong capital levels. The credit union reports a net worth ratio of 24.19%, representing an increase of 358.71 bps from the prior period, and a solvency ratio of 1.53%. This robust buffer provides ample room for absorbing potential losses while maintaining regulatory standing.

Asset quality remains stable and manageable. The delinquency ratio stands at 1.53% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at 0.81% of average loans. Delinquency has improved by 96.80 bps from the prior period, reflecting a marked reduction in non-performing assets.

Profitability remains positive. The credit union reports a return on average assets (ROAA) of 0.81% and a net interest margin of 2.76%, which rose by 49.88 bps from the prior period, driven by improved earning-asset performance. Net operating expenses to average assets decreased to 1.84%, supporting overall profitability.

Balance-sheet structure continues to reflect an adequate operating profile. Loans represent 42.88% of shares and have declined by 30.74%. Shares and deposits have also contracted, declining by 35.35%, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction driven by membership attrition. The core funding ratio stands at a healthy 57.21%, demonstrating that the institution maintains sufficient liquidity despite this shrinkage.

Overall, BOPTI enters Q1'26 with solid capital and profitability but faces headwinds from shrinking deposits. Membership retention will be critical for sustaining performance.
